seoTitle: How to Print A4 Size in Canva Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to print A4 size designs in Canva, from setting up the right document size to exporting a print-ready file.
intro: Getting your document sized and exported correctly is essential when you need an A4 print, whether it's a flyer, brochure, or invitation. Canva handles standard paper sizes well once you know where to set them. This guide covers setting up and printing an A4 design.

steps.heading: Set up an A4 document
steps.body: When creating a new design, choose A4 from the size presets, or enter custom dimensions of 210 x 297 mm.

steps.heading: Design within the printable margins
steps.body: Keep important text and graphics away from the edges to avoid them being cut off during printing.

steps.heading: Check for print-quality images
steps.body: Make sure any photos or graphics are high resolution so they don't look blurry once printed at full size.

steps.heading: Download as a print-ready PDF
steps.body: Export your design as 'PDF Print' rather than standard PDF to ensure the best quality for printing.

steps.heading: Print or send to a print shop
steps.body: Print at home on A4 paper, or send the file to a professional printer if you need higher volume.

faq.question: What's the difference between PDF Standard and PDF Print in Canva?
faq.answer: PDF Print produces a higher-resolution file suited for physical printing, while PDF Standard is optimized for smaller file size and screen viewing.

faq.question: Can I print A4 designs at home?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as your printer supports A4 paper, which is standard in most regions outside North America.

faq.question: Do I need Canva Pro to export print-ready files?
faq.answer: No, PDF Print export is available on Canva's free plan.

faq.question: What if my printer only supports Letter size?
faq.answer: You can resize your Canva design to Letter dimensions, but be aware the layout proportions will shift slightly.
